# Review Videos as a Resource to Prepare for Quizzes

<!-- This is a comment. Below, you'll see code for inserting an image. To make this image appear, update <custom-path>. To add an image, save it inside the imgs folder of this repository. -->
<img src="<custom-path>/static/imgs/logo.png" alt="Image of Comp110 rainbow logo. "  width="500"/>

## Analysis Summary

In this project I analyzed data that COMP110 students provided in a survey to help find ways to improve this class. My analysis focused specifically on whether or not having short review videos covering key topics in this course would be beneficial to students when studying for quizzes. My analysis focused on three variables tested in the survey: lesson video effectiveness, support for pre-lecture videos, and quiz preparation effectiveness. I used scatterplots, histograms, and bar charts to visualize and better understand the relationship between these variables and whether or not adding review videos would benefit the majority of students.

## Visualizations

### 1: Relationship Between Video Effectiveness and Quiz Prep

### 2. Support for Pre-Lecture Videos
<img src="/ex09project/static/imgs/plot2.png" alt="Pre-lecture video support chart" width="600"/>

### 3. Average Quiz Preparation Effectiveness
<img src="/ex09project/static/imgs/plot3.png" alt="Quiz preparation effectiveness chart" width="600"/>

## Conclusions

Based on the visualizations it can be concluded that:

1. There is a general positive trend between quiz prep effectiveness and lesson video effectiveness, which proves that watching videos is an effective way to review for a quiz.

2. The majority of students rate their agreement with pre-lecture videos a 5, 6, or 7 on a scale of 1-7, showing general support for videos that aid in learning or reviewing.

3. Agreement with pre-lecture videos doesn't necessarily determine quiz prep effectiveness, showing that while some students benefit from review videos, others might have different preferred study methods.

Overall, the data supports the addition of review videos as a resource for students to use. These videos would create value for studetns by aiding them in their quiz preparation and helping strengthen their understanding of the main course concepts in COMP110!
